nodes.label: World Model (Map, Phase, Depth Axes)
nodes.rationale: Map is the primary exploration unit. Siren phase controls which maps are accessible. Depth is the vertical axis within each map. Scene switches when traveling between maps. This three-axis model (map, phase, depth) defines the entire world progression.

nodes.label: Siren Phase Lifecycle
nodes.rationale: Six phases: Ovo/Choco (initial, outside map logic), Bebê (first free phase), Criança, Adolescente, Jovem, Adulta (full access). Phase eligibility is cumulative (current + all previous). Progression follows the existing in-game system.

nodes.label: Map Access and Discovery Rules
nodes.rationale: Phase gives cumulative map eligibility but does not auto-discover maps. Only Recife Tropical starts as known. Every other map requires: hint → follow hint → confirm travel. Future-phase maps appear locked in menu. Returning to lower-phase maps is always allowed.

nodes.label: Depth Layer System (7 Layers)
nodes.rationale: Seven depth layers: Clara, Rasa, Média, Azul, Profunda, Abissal, Superfície. All exist in every map; what varies is unlock state. Unlock criteria: phase minimum (global), adaptation (global per-layer), special discoveries (per-map). Hybrid global/per-map unlock model.

nodes.label: Minimap System (Terraria-Inspired)
nodes.rationale: Inspired by Terraria's minimap: fog of war, gradient revelation by proximity, persistence per save, fixed vision radius by phase (smaller for baby, maximum for adult). Shows siren position, revealed terrain, depth zones, discovered POIs. Vertical orientation with Y=depth, X=horizontal. Not a HUD element; appears in expedition map screen.

nodes.label: Points of Interest (POI) System
nodes.rationale: Each map+depth combination has its own POIs placed at random coordinates. Types: narrativo, minigame, npc, evento, coleta. Undiscovered POIs show as silhouettes. Discovery is organic (proximity-based). Return to discovered POIs is clickable. V1 maps will not implement POIs but architecture must be ready. Data-driven design: each POI defined in config file, new POI = new data entry, no code changes.

nodes.label: RewardSystem
nodes.rationale: Unified reward system independent of source (POI, random event, NPC, minigame). All rewards pass through RewardSystem. Supports: conchas, temporary pets, temporary effects (no hunger, faster swimming), items, storytelling. Temporary effects use a buff system with timer.

nodes.label: Map Travel System
nodes.rationale: Travel between maps triggers scene load. Steps: select map → validate phase access → scene transition → save current map+position → load destination scene → place siren at entry point. Each map is a separate GameScene; no continuous multi-map world in memory.

nodes.label: Conchas (Shell) Economy
nodes.rationale: Game currency. Minigames convert points to conchas through economic profiles. Base implementation in GameBalance. Detailed criteria in docs/BALANCEAMENTO_MINIGAMES.md.

nodes.label: Entry Text Storytelling System
nodes.rationale: On first entry to a new map or depth, display floating storytelling text. Light, non-blocking. Content varies by mapId + profundidade. Triggered once, flagged by primeiraVisita per save.

nodes.label: Offline Movement System
nodes.rationale: Siren continues exploring when app is closed. On reopen, trace organic path between close position and current position, apply map revelation along path. POIs passed during offline are visible and clickable but rewards only occur when player is watching.

nodes.label: Map Discovery Flow
nodes.rationale: New maps discovered through random events or POIs. Player receives map fragment → clicks to follow → siren traces path → area shows visual preview of next map → siren shows curiosity → confirmation button → new scene loads. Randomness decides when and from which source, not which map (follows progression order).

nodes.label: Coragem (Courage) — Removed System
nodes.rationale: Decision: removed from the system for now. Not an unlock criterion or active attribute. May be revisited if narrative need arises.
